title: Most AI agents are fake (here's a 10-second test)
summary: Jeff Su argues that most companies falsely market their products as AI agents. A simple test distinguishes real agents: they generate novel text, images, or video; products that merely classify or output probabilities are not generative AI. Examples include Gmail's non-agentic spam filter versus Google Docs' generative "Help me write" and truly agentic systems like Google's Antigravity and Anthropic's Cowork.
